Chinese: 我还没有听过她跟别人说意大利语。
Pinyin: Wǒ hái méiyǒu tīngguo tā gēn biérén shuō Yìdàlìhuà.
English: I haven't heard her speak Italian with other people yet.

Pinyin: Tā hěn shǎo yǒu jīhuì tīng hé shuō Yìdàlìyǔ.
English: She rarely has the opportunity to hear and speak Italian.

Chinese: 你有用 WhatsApp 跟他们聊天吗？
Pinyin: Nǐ yǒu yòng WhatsApp gēn tāmen liáotiān ma?

Chinese: 你有跟他们打视频电话吗？
Pinyin: Nǐ yǒu gēn tāmen dǎ shìpín diànhuà ma?

English: to follow / to go together with someone
verb + 着 pattern — describes a continuing state or ongoing posture
to be wearing (glasses / hat / watch / accessories)

Pinyin: Zhuōzi shàng fàngzhe hěn duō běn shū.
English: There are many books on the table.
The TV might also be off.
I haven't turned on the TV yet.

Pinyin: Qiáng shàng guàzhe yì fú huà.
English: A painting is hanging on the wall.
contrast — 跟着 = you follow someone else; 带着 = you lead / bring someone along
to take someone along / to bring someone with you (you lead)

Chinese: 参观的时候你带着他去，他不认识那边。
Pinyin: Cānguān de shíhou nǐ dàizhe tā qù; tā bù rènshi nàbiān.
English: Take him with you during the visit — he isn't familiar with that place.

Chinese: 参观的时候你带着他去。
